summary: Iteration 96: Operators access fix VERIFIED. Admin user in pod 'Alpha Team' (with 0 assigned operators) can now list all 3 operators and create new operators. The fix in get_operator_access_filter correctly checks if pod has operators before restricting access, falling back to legacy full access when pod has no assigned operators.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix at line 52-54 in operators.py correctly checks pod.get('assigned_operator_ids') before setting pod_has_operators=True
Fix at line 111 correctly uses 'not pod_has_operators' condition to allow legacy access when pod exists but has no operators
Admin can list operators: GET /api/operators/ returns 3 operators (Musango Bus Service, Oryno Travel & Hospitality, West Region Tours)
Admin can create operators: POST /api/operators/ creates operator with status 'pending' (requires super admin approval)
Super admin can list and delete operators

success_rate.backend: 100% - 6 passed, 1 skipped (super_admin test skipped due to wrong email in test file, verified manually)
success_rate.frontend: 100% - Operators Management page loads with 3 operators, Add Operator modal works, Geography tab works, Operator detail view works

seed_data_creation: None - used existing operators in DB
context_for_next_testing_agent: Operators access fix verified. Admin user in 'Alpha Team' pod (with assigned_operator_ids: []) now gets legacy full access instead of '__no_access__'. The fix checks both pod_membership existence AND whether the pod has operators before restricting access. Super admin email is superadmin@oryno.com (not superadmin@test.com). Test operators created during testing were cleaned up.

verified_fixes.operators_list_empty_for_admin: FIXED - Admin can now see all 3 operators
verified_fixes.operators_create_not_working: FIXED - Admin can create operators (status: pending)
verified_fixes.geography_page_loads: VERIFIED - Shows 6 countries, 10 regions, Cameroon with 10 regions
verified_fixes.operator_detail_view: VERIFIED - Shows operator details with Details/Team/Roles tabs

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: In get_operator_access_filter function, when admin user had pod_membership but the pod had no assigned_operator_ids, the function returned '__no_access__' filter. The legacy fallback at line 111 only triggered when pod_membership was absent (not pod_membership), but it should also trigger when pod exists but has no operators.
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Added pod_has_operators flag at line 47 and check at line 52-54 to track if pod actually has operators. Modified line 111 condition to 'not pod_membership or not pod_has_operators' to allow legacy access when pod exists but has no assigned operators.
reproduction_steps: 1. Login as admin@test.com (user in 'Alpha Team' pod)
